commit.message:
Split x64/arm64 builds into parallel pipeline stages (#14497)

* Split x64/arm64 builds into parallel pipeline stages

Restructure the CI/CD pipeline to build x64 and arm64 in parallel
instead of sequentially, reducing end-to-end build time.

Pipeline shapes:
- PR: build_x64 ∥ build_arm64 → test (uses installer.msix directly)
- Nightly: build_x64 ∥ build_arm64 → package → test (dev-cert bundle)
- Release: build_x64 ∥ build_arm64 → package → test (ESRP-signed bundle)

Key changes:
- Extract shared build-job.yml template parameterized by platform
- Add package-stage.yml that creates msixbundle from both platform
  artifacts, ESRP-signs for release, dev-cert signs for nightly
- PR tests run immediately after x64 build using installer.msix
  (no package stage, no bundle needed)
- Release/nightly tests wait for the package stage and test the real
  signed bundle that gets published
- CloudTest configs are parameterized: release tests pull the bundle
  from the [package] artifact, PR tests use installer.msix from [drop]
- arm64 + formatting checks always run in parallel with x64 but don't
  block the PR test gate
- CodeQL runs in the arm64 stage (off the critical path)
- flight-stage and nuget-stage updated for new stage names

* formatting

* Remove redundant runtime conditions on release-only tasks

These tasks are already wrapped in compile-time conditionals which
prevent them from being added to the pipeline definition for
non-release builds. The runtime condition checks can never evaluate
to false at that point, so they are pure noise.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback from OneBlue

- Remove wslcsdk NuGet staging (wslc is not in master)
- Always include [package] provider in TestMap.xml.in instead of
  conditionally injecting via PACKAGE_PROVIDER_BLOCK cmake variable.
  PR builds simply use [drop] as TEST_PACKAGE_PROVIDER; the [package]
  provider exists but is unused.
- Add BUNDLE_ONLY cmake option so the package pipeline stage reuses
  cmake's existing bundle target instead of forking makeappx logic.
  This locks the SDK version via cmake (CMAKE_SYSTEM_VERSION) and
  keeps bundle creation logic in one place (msixinstaller/CMakeLists.txt).
  The pipeline now copies msix files to expected paths, runs a fast
  cmake configure with -DBUNDLE_ONLY=TRUE, and builds the bundle target.
- Remove dead NuGet binary restore step in package stage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback

- Fix PACKAGE_VERSION regex: escape dots so only A.B.C.D is accepted
- Consolidate CMake defaults (build type, config types, output dir)
  before BUNDLE_ONLY block to avoid duplication
- Nightly tests now use the full bundle from the package stage instead
  of installer.msix (new INCLUDE_PACKAGE_STAGE cmake variable)
- Package stage reuses version output from build stage instead of
  recomputing it

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Ben Hillis <benhill@ntdev.microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add copilot instructions for Azure Search MCP defaults

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace pre-commit hook with CMake-generated clang-format check (#40136)

* Replace pre-commit hook with CMake-generated clang-format check

Replace the old pre-commit hook that shelled out to PowerShell and
never blocked commits (-NoFail) with a CMake-generated hook that
calls clang-format directly on staged C/C++ files.

- Add tools/hooks/pre-commit.in as a CMake template
- CMake resolves the clang-format path at configure time via
  LLVM_INSTALL_DIR, matching the existing FormatSource.ps1.in pattern
- Hook blocks commits on formatting errors, skips gracefully if
  clang-format is not available (cmake not yet run)
- ~5x faster than the old PowerShell approach (~0.5s vs ~2.6s)

* Make pre-commit hook behavior configurable via WSL_PRE_COMMIT_MODE

Add WSL_PRE_COMMIT_MODE CMake cache variable with three modes:
- warn (default): report formatting issues without blocking commit
- error: block commit when formatting issues are found
- fix: auto-format files and re-stage them

Also addresses PR feedback:
- Generate hook into build tree, copy to source tree for out-of-source builds
- Use repo-local tools/clang-format.exe instead of LLVM_INSTALL_DIR path
- Use @ONLY in configure_file to avoid shell variable substitution issues
- Document modes in dev-loop.md and UserConfig.cmake.sample

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Ben Hillis <benhill@ntdev.microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add client-side rate limiter to UptimeRobot API client (#152)

* Initial plan

* Add client-side API rate limiter to prevent quota exhaustion

Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/joelp172/uptime-robot-operator/sessions/7171f0e9-6419-4444-8650-0ba93f5d659d

* fix: move rate limiter wait into retry loop and share limiter per API key

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/joelp172/uptime-robot-operator/sessions/baf45845-c54e-4268-a648-07532f869ef6

Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: improve rate limiter robustness, error reporting, and test reliability

Add stderr warnings when UPTIME_ROBOT_RATE_LIMIT, UPTIME_ROBOT_MAX_RETRIES,
and UPTIME_ROBOT_BASE_DELAY env vars are set to invalid values instead of
silently falling back to defaults. Hash API keys in the global limiter
registry to avoid holding secrets as plaintext map keys. Simplify
getSharedLimiter to a single atomic LoadOrStore call.

Harden tests with resetGlobalLimiters cleanup, wider timing tolerances for
CI, boundary value subtests (zero, negative, float), and a nil limiter
guard test. Update comments and docs to clarify burst=rate relationship.

* fix(e2e): match JSON-formatted controller-runtime log output in metrics test

The metrics endpoint test asserts that controller logs contain
'controller-runtime.metrics\tServing metrics server' (tab-delimited text
format), but the controller outputs JSON structured logs where this appears
as 'msg': 'Serving metrics server'. The substring never matches, causing a
120s timeout that cascades into the webhook test failing because AfterAll
deletes the namespace before the next test suite runs.

Accept both text and JSON log formats using SatisfyAny.

* refactor: parse env vars once via sync.Once, use full hash, add Load fast path

Address review comments:
- Wrap env var parsing in sync.Once so warnings for invalid values are
  emitted once at startup instead of on every per-reconcile NewClient call.
- Use the full SHA-256 digest in limiterKey instead of truncating to 8
  bytes, eliminating collision risk between different API keys.
- Re-add Load fast path in getSharedLimiter before LoadOrStore to avoid
  allocating a rate.Limiter on every NewClient call when one already exists.
- Add resetClientConfig test helper and call it in subtests so each test
  gets a fresh sync.Once for env var parsing.

* fix: read UPTIME_ROBOT_API on every NewClient call instead of caching

The sync.Once refactor inadvertently cached UPTIME_ROBOT_API alongside the
numeric/duration env vars. Controller tests change this env var between
reconciles to simulate API failures, so caching it caused 6 test failures
where expected errors became nil. Keep UPTIME_ROBOT_API read per-call while
only caching the parsed numeric settings that benefit from sync.Once.

* fix(e2e): reset e2eInfraReady after Manager AfterAll tears down infrastructure

The Manager test suite's AfterAll undeploys the operator and deletes the
namespace, but never resets e2eInfraReady. The webhook test suite's
BeforeAll calls ensureE2EInfra() which returns immediately because the
flag is still true, then fails creating resources in the deleted namespace.

* fix: remove codeql workflow

* fix: close resp.Body in rate limiter tests to prevent resource leaks

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Joel Pinder <joel.pinder@adarga.ai>

commit.message:
[#305] Implement Gazebo robot MCP server (#325)

## Summary

Implement a robot-focused FastMCP server for the Dr.QP Gazebo
environment. This adds host-side and devcontainer-side control paths for
booting and shutting down the robot, reading robot and world state, and
recording robot state snapshots over time.

## Changes

### Features
- Add a new `drqp_robot_mcp` Python package that exposes a FastMCP
server for robot lifecycle and simulation inspection workflows.
- Add MCP tools to boot the robot to `torque_on`, shut it down through
the lifecycle state machine, read the latest robot state, record state
snapshots, and fetch Gazebo world entity poses.
- Register the new MCP server in the workspace MCP configuration so it
can be launched from the repository virtual environment.

### Configuration
- Add package metadata and console entrypoint so the MCP server can be
installed and run from the existing Python environment.
- Add the new package to the repository requirements so editable
installs include the MCP server.

### Tests
- Add unit coverage for the host-side controller lifecycle and recording
behavior.
- Add unit coverage for parsing Gazebo pose output into structured
world-state data.

### Documentation
- Add package README covering installation, startup, and the supported
MCP capabilities.

## Technical Details

### Approach
- The implementation is split into a host-side controller and a
devcontainer-side bridge.
- The host-side controller is responsible for starting the devcontainer
service, invoking bridge commands, coordinating lifecycle transitions,
and managing in-memory robot state recording.
- The bridge runs inside the devcontainer so ROS 2 and Gazebo operations
can execute in the environment where those tools are available.
- Robot state is assembled from ROS topics, while world state is
assembled from Gazebo pose output plus the ROS clock.

### Dependencies
- Add `mcp[cli]>=1.26.0` for the FastMCP server and CLI runtime.

### Assumptions
1. The existing devcontainer compose service is the correct execution
environment for ROS 2 and Gazebo operations.
2. The robot lifecycle can be driven through the existing `/robot_event`
and `/robot_state` topics.
3. Running the MCP server from the repository virtual environment is the
intended local workflow.

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
- Added controller tests covering boot-up, shutdown, and state recording
flows.
- Added parser tests covering Gazebo pose message extraction.

### Integration and CI
- PR checks currently show successful docs, validation, reformat, and
Codecov runs.
- An additional `Agent` check is still in progress.

### Manual Testing
- Not documented in the branch changes.
- I have not run this PR branch locally from the current workspace
session because the local checkout is on a different branch.

## Breaking Changes

No breaking changes expected.

## Risk Assessment

### Risks
- The MCP server depends on Docker Compose, the devcontainer service,
and ROS/Gazebo tooling being available and healthy at runtime.
- Lifecycle transitions assume the current robot state machine topics
and event names remain stable.

### Mitigation
- The controller raises explicit errors when container commands fail or
when expected state transitions do not occur.
- Unit tests cover the controller flow and the Gazebo pose parsing logic
to reduce regression risk in the core orchestration code.

## Related

- Closes #305

## Checklist

- [x] Code follows project conventions
- [x] Tests added for new logic
- [x] Documentation updated
- [x] No breaking changes expected
- [ ] Manual runtime verification completed against a live Gazebo
session

---------

Signed-off-by: Anton Matosov <anton.matosov@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Anton Matosov <anton-matosov@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: anton-matosov <3339485+anton-matosov@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: split monolithic CI/CD pipeline into three workflows (#26)

Split the single Build/Test/Release workflow (ci.yml) into three
separate, focused workflows:

1. ci.yml (Build/Test) -- runs on PRs, pushes to main, and manual
   dispatch. Handles validation, linting, testing, security scanning,
   image builds, plugin builds, and multi-arch manifests. Images are
   pushed to GHCR only on push to main. The version job uses git
   describe instead of bump logic. The release job and all release-
   related inputs (bump, draft, prerelease, dry_run) are removed.

2. prepare-release.yml (Prepare Release) -- manual workflow_dispatch
   with a bump level input (patch/minor/major). Computes the next
   semver from the latest tag, creates a release/v{version} branch,
   inserts the version heading into CHANGELOG.md, and opens a PR
   to main.

3. release.yml (Release) -- triggers via workflow_run after Build/Test
   completes on main. Checks if the merge came from a release/v*
   branch; if so, extracts the version from CHANGELOG.md, creates
   and pushes the git tag, re-tags per-arch images with the release
   version using imagetools, re-renders Kubernetes manifests with
   proper image references, downloads plugin binaries from the CI
   run, generates and signs an SBOM, and creates a draft GitHub
   release.

This separation provides clearer responsibility boundaries: CI runs
on every code change, release preparation is an explicit human-
triggered step, and the actual release is fully automated after the
release PR merges.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: production-grade dual-ML pipeline with full dashboard integration

Pipeline upgrades:
- ADC Corrector now fully functional with training pipeline
- Realistic ADC non-linearity simulation (INL + DNL errors)
- LSTM model upgraded with output projection layer and multi-layer support
- Dynamic threshold saved to JSON and loaded by agent (no hardcoded values)
- Data preprocessing uses anomaly labels instead of hardcoded row indices

Live agent rewrite:
- Removed hardcoded API key (now reads from .env)
- Both ML stages active with full metadata sent to dashboard
- Publishes rawValue, correctedValue, reconstructionError, threshold, pipeline status
- Structured logging, graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM
- Centralized config.py for all hyperparameters and paths

Cleanup:
- Removed committed __pycache__, lightning_logs, checkpoints, data CSVs, plot PNGs
- Added .gitignore to prevent re-committing generated artifacts
- Removed unused dependencies (seaborn, torchvision), added missing ones (ably, dotenv)
- Added .env.example for onboarding
- Complete README with architecture, data flow, and setup guide

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(interp): add global epoch guard to prototype-chain IC

Cherry-picked from agent commit 408fb4bd.
Adds ProtoLoadIc.proto_global_epoch field and probe_epoch() method
that skips expensive proto_generation() call when the global epoch
is unchanged, reducing per-access overhead in prototype chain lookups.

commit.message:
docs(spec): document collection type-boundary restrictions (#886)

- §3.3.1: note that [T] slice annotations are rejected; only [T; N] fixed
  arrays are supported (MLIR has no slice lowering)
- §3.7.5 (Rc): add bullet that Rc<T> cannot appear as a collection element
  or key type in Vec, HashMap, or HashSet
- §3.10.3 (Vec): add element-restriction note — Rc<T> elements and element
  types that structurally contain fixed-size arrays are rejected
- §3.10.3 (HashMap): extend implementation boundary note to cover Rc<T>
  rejection in key and value positions
- §3.10.4 (HashSet): extend shipped-collections note to cover Rc<T> element
  rejection

All changes reflect current checker behaviour introduced in the type-boundary
hardening work; no speculative future support is described.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: migrate database from SQLite to MongoDB (#18)

## Summary

Migrates the backend from SQLite (SQLAlchemy) to MongoDB (Motor async),
fixes architecture and coding standards violations identified in a
project review against `REQUIREMENTS.md` and `copilot-instructions.md`.

## Changes

### 🔴 Database migration (MongoDB)
- Replace `SQLAlchemy`/`aiosqlite` with `motor==3.4.0` +
`pymongo==4.6.2` in `pyproject.toml`
- Rewrite `adapters/db/session.py`: async Motor client, reads
`MONGODB_URI` env var
- Rewrite `adapters/db/repository.py`: fully async `LinkRepository`
using Motor
- Delete `adapters/db/models.py` (SQLAlchemy ORM no longer needed)
- Update `docker-compose.yaml`: add `mongo:7` service, replace
`SQLITE_DB_PATH` with `MONGODB_URI`

### 🏗️ Clean Architecture fix
- Add `domain/ports.py` with `LinkRepositoryPort` Protocol — adapters
now implement a domain interface
- All API route handlers `await` async repository calls
- `lifespan()` creates MongoDB indexes instead of SQL tables

### 🟡 Standards compliance
- Remove `from __future__ import annotations` from 4 domain modules
(prohibited by coding standards)
- Fix `TOKEN_PEPPER` env var mismatch between `docker-compose.yaml` and
`deps.py`
- Add `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: lint (ruff + mypy), tests (pytest),
and frontend build on push/PR

### 🎨 Frontend — Svelte 5
- Upgrade `svelte` from `^4.2.18` to `^5.0.0` and
`@sveltejs/vite-plugin-svelte` to `^4.0.0`
- Migrate `App.svelte` to Svelte 5 runes: `$state()`, `onclick`
(replaces `on:click`), typed error handling

### 🧪 Tests
- Rewrite `conftest.py` with `mongomock-motor` in-process MongoDB mock
- Rename + rewrite `test_repository_sqlite.py` →
`test_repository_mongo.py` (async)
- Update `test_id_token.py`: `generate_unique_link_id` is now `async`
- Update `test_api_crud.py`: use `override_db` fixture, align env var
name

## Test results
```
29 passed in 0.19s
```
Ruff and mypy both pass with zero errors.

## Out of scope (future work)
- Redis caching layer
- Rate limiting (429)
- Domain blocklist / abuse controls  
- `robots.txt` endpoint
- Extracting use-cases layer from `app.py`

---------

Co-authored-by: Jaroslaw Wencel <jaroslaw@wencel.it>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix missing KO moves: check HP before applying hpState in filter

The post-processing filter was applying hpState BEFORE checking if the
target was already fainted. Since hpState for a KO move shows the target
at 0 HP, the filter incorrectly treated the KO move itself as hitting
an already-dead target and removed it.

Fix: check target HP using tracked state from PREVIOUS entries, then
apply the current entry's hpState for subsequent checks.

Result: 100% move retention (1338/1338 raw moves in parsed log).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address security review findings

- auth.js: restructure protect() to check for token header before try/catch,
  eliminating unreachable code and clarifying flow
- applications.js: cast jobId to String before Mongoose query to prevent
  NoSQL injection
- jobs.js: whitelist allowed fields in PATCH update instead of passing
  raw req.body to findByIdAndUpdate
- index.js: add general API rate limiter (300 req/15min) covering all
  /api routes to address missing rate-limiting alerts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Skandeerkefi <94742678+Skandeerkefi@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve all Biome CI lint errors

- Sort imports/exports across 75 auto-fixed files (biome lint:fix)
- Replace array index keys with stable keys:
  - ActionCenter.tsx: item.label
  - SeoBasicsSection.tsx: check.label
  - TrafficCharts.tsx: entry.country
  - UpdatesSection.tsx: u.currentVersion+newVersion, p.slug, th.slug
- StatTile.tsx: remove unsupported aria-label from decorative span,
  add aria-hidden (Tooltip already provides accessible label)
- CommandPalette.tsx: add biome-ignore for two div[role=button] cases
  that wrap nested interactive elements (cannot use <button>)
- navbar/index.tsx: add biome-ignore for dangerouslySetInnerHTML
  (trusted WordPress admin bar HTML from server)

All 175 tests pass. Biome CI: 0 errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix PE scaling: evaluate resources before grabbing tasks, include PE workers in deferral logic

- Move EvaluateResourceNeedsAsync BEFORE WorkOnOwnTasksAsync in PE loop
- Call EvaluateResourceNeedsAsync even during PR implementation (before continue)
- Update deferral logic to include non-leader PE workers, not just SE/JE
- Fix EvaluateResourceNeedsAsync to check actual pool capacity before requesting spawns
- Revert SE/JE pool defaults to 0 (PE-only scaling by design)

commit.message:
Fix `usethis tool` failing in directories with leading dots (#1903)

* Initial plan

* Fix uv/poetry init failing in directories with leading dots in their name

Pass --name with a sanitized project name to uv init, and fix
get_project_name_from_dir() to strip leading/trailing non-alphanumeric
characters (dots, dashes, underscores) per PEP packaging spec.

Also update poetry init to use get_project_name_from_dir() instead of
the raw directory name for consistency.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/usethis-python/usethis-python/sessions/e415c235-da21-4847-b92c-704513c59e6e

Co-authored-by: nathanjmcdougall <18602289+nathanjmcdougall@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Windows test failure: use PurePosixPath instead of real directories

The test_only_dots test created a directory named "..." which on Windows
is interpreted as parent directory traversal (../..), causing
FileExistsError. Since get_project_name_from_dir() only reads Path.name
and never accesses the filesystem, all tests now use PurePosixPath
synthetic paths instead of creating real directories.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/usethis-python/usethis-python/sessions/b47fb40c-7e7c-44f9-89e3-258b10a5fe34

Co-authored-by: nathanjmcdougall <18602289+nathanjmcdougall@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix basedpyright: replace PurePosixPath with Path/str in tests

PurePosixPath is not assignable to Path | str | None. Use tmp_path /
"name" (Path without mkdir) for most tests, and string paths for
Windows-problematic names ("..." and "project.") since usethis_config
.set() accepts str and converts to Path internally.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/usethis-python/usethis-python/sessions/06111449-4dd0-43b5-87cf-f13293af9a57

Co-authored-by: nathanjmcdougall <18602289+nathanjmcdougall@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: nathanjmcdougall <18602289+nathanjmcdougall@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add 10 new features to MailMerge-Pro

1. Email Templates Library - Save/load/delete templates with 3 built-in
   templates (Welcome, Product Update, Invoice) in collapsible Step 3 panel
2. Scheduled Sending - Date/time picker with countdown timer, cancel support,
   interrupted schedule detection via localStorage
3. Email Tracking - Checkbox that enables Graph API read receipts with
   upgrade note for advanced tracking
4. A/B Testing - Toggle enables dual subject/body editors with tabbed UI,
   configurable split ratios (50/50, 70/30, 80/20), deterministic splitting
5. Contact Groups/Segments - Save/load/delete/merge recipient lists in
   localStorage with 3MB size warning
6. HTML Template Import - File picker and drag-and-drop for .html files,
   auto-detects merge field placeholders
7. Signature Auto-Insert - Fetch from Outlook via Graph API or paste
   manually, auto-append toggle persisted in localStorage
8. Rate Limit Dashboard - Daily sent counter with color-coded progress bar,
   auto-suggested delay based on recipient count
9. Multi-Language Support (i18n) - 6 languages (EN/ES/FR/DE/PT/JA) with
   data-i18n attributes and t() translation function
10. Admin Dashboard - Campaign analytics with stats grid, monthly CSS bar
    chart, top recipients, and recent campaigns list
